name: The vineyards of Almendralejo — circular through the Tierra de Barros
name_original: Los viñedos de Almendralejo — circular por la Tierra de Barros
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Almendralejo traverse a landscape characterized by extensive vineyards, particularly within the Tierra de Barros region. The area features rolling terrain with quiet roads, offering varied scenery for cyclists. Notable natural features include the Embalse de Alange, a large reservoir that forms a focal point for longer routes. The region's topography provides a mix of flat sections through agricultural lands and gentle ascents across its undulating hills. Best touring cycling routes around Almendralejo The most popular touring cycling route is Almendralejo and Embalse de Alange, a 48.5 miles (78.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Alange Reservoir and broader natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is The vineyards of Almendralejo — circular through the Tierra de Barros, an easy 15.1 miles (24.3 km) path. This route leads through extensive vineyards on sand tracks, offering scenic views. Local touring cyclists also love the Plaza de Toros de Almendralejo – La Vaguada Castle loop from Almendralejo, a 26.1 miles (42.0 km) trail leading through rolling terrain and past historical sites, often completed in about 3 hours 38 minutes. Touring cycling around Almendralejo is defined by vineyards, rolling terrain, and the Embalse de Alange. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Almendralejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 170 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Almendralejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Almendralejo?
A: There are nearly 50 touring cycling routes around Almendralejo, offering a diverse range of options for cyclists. These include 8 easy routes, 37 moderate routes, and 4 more challenging options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Almendralejo?
A: Touring cycling routes in Almendralejo primarily traverse the extensive vineyards of the Tierra de Barros region, often on sand tracks. You'll also encounter rolling terrain with quiet roads, offering varied scenery. Longer routes may feature the broader natural landscapes around the Embalse de Alange, a significant reservoir.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Almendralejo offers 8 easy touring cycling routes. A particularly pleasant option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022467158/the-vineyards-of-almendralejo-circular-through-the-tierra-de-barros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The vineyards of Almendralejo — circular through the Tierra de Barros&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 24.3 km path that leads through scenic vineyards.

Q: What are the best times of year to go touring cycling in Almendralejo?
A: The best times to visit Almendralejo for touring cycling are typically from March to June and from September to November. These periods avoid the intense summer heat, offering more comfortable temperatures for longer rides.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of the extensive vineyards of the Ribera del Guadiana. Longer tours often feature the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alange&lt;/a&gt;, a large reservoir. You might also encounter historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2372869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Castle&lt;/a&gt;, perched atop Culebra hill, or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4378780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Nuestra Señora de la Purificación&lt;/a&gt; in Almendralejo.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022467158/the-vineyards-of-almendralejo-circular-through-the-tierra-de-barros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The vineyards of Almendralejo — circular through the Tierra de Barros&lt;/a&gt; is an easy circular route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Toros de Almendralejo – La Vaguada Castle loop from Almendralejo&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through rolling terrain and past historical sites.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Almendralejo?
A: The touring cycling routes in Almendralejo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the scenic rides through extensive vineyards, the quiet roads, and the varied terrain that caters to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging touring routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, there are 4 difficult touring cycling routes available. A notable challenging option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353823696/almendralejo-and-embalse-de-alange" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almendralejo and Embalse de Alange&lt;/a&gt;, a 78.1 km trail that offers extensive views of the reservoir and broader natural landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through historical sites or towns?
A: Absolutely. Many routes incorporate historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Toros de Almendralejo – La Vaguada Castle loop from Almendralejo&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads past historical sites. The wider Extremadura region, where Almendralejo is located, is rich in historic towns and villages, and the Camino de Santiago also passes through the area, adding a cultural dimension.

Q: What are the typical elevation changes on touring routes in Almendralejo?
A: The region features rolling terrain, so you can expect a mix of flat sections through agricultural lands and gentle ascents across undulating hills. For example, the easy 'Vineyards of Almendralejo' route has about 100 meters of elevation gain, while the more challenging 'Almendralejo and Embalse de Alange' route features over 600 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Embalse de Alange?
A: Yes, the Embalse de Alange is a significant natural feature in the region and is a focal point for several routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353823696/almendralejo-and-embalse-de-alange" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almendralejo and Embalse de Alange&lt;/a&gt; route specifically offers extensive views of this impressive reservoir.
tips:
Enjoy a pleasant walk along the sand track among the vineyards of the area. It is the only crop that can be seen from miles away.

On rainy days, it is recommended to use the national road as an alternative, since the track can be very muddy and can make pedaling difficult.

Almendralejo, located in the province of Badajoz, Extremadura, is a city with a rich historical, cultural, and agricultural heritage.

Founded after the Reconquista, around the 13th century, by the Order of Santiago.

Its name comes from the almond trees that once covered the original site.

By the 16th century, it already had a well-established urban structure.

In 1513, it received its charter of privileges, which spurred its development.

waypoints.name: Church of Nuestra Señora de la Purificación (Almendralejo)
